novel_features.name: Local retail index sync
novel_features.description: Pull products (and optional price history) from one or more retailers into a fast local SQLite store so every other query runs instantly and offline.
novel_features.rationale: Turns N single-retailer API calls into one joinable local dataset that the per-retailer live API can never assemble in a single request.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this first: the compare/deals/arbitrage/analytics commands all read what index populates.

novel_features.name: Cross-retailer identifier compare
novel_features.description: See which synced retailer sells the exact same item (by UPC/EAN/GTIN/ASIN) cheapest right now, ranked, with the savings spread.
novel_features.rationale: The live API is keyed per retailer and cannot join one identifier across 70 storefronts; only the local store holds all retailers' rows side by side.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it to answer 'who has this cheapest' in one call instead of N per-retailer lookups.

novel_features.name: Compound deal finder
novel_features.description: Surface deals that clear several bars at once — deep discount, under a price ceiling, well-reviewed, in stock — across all tracked retailers in one ranked list.
novel_features.rationale: Joins filters across retailers that no single API call combines (the paid endpoint is one retailer at a time; the free one drops several filters).
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs the best deals worth a human's time, not a raw product dump.

novel_features.name: FBA arbitrage margin
novel_features.description: Rank synced products by what you would net reselling them on Amazon after referral and FBA fees, filtered by ROI and buy price.
novel_features.rationale: Profitability requires joining a retailer's buy price to Amazon's fee/profitability block — two responses about the same item — which only the local store holds together on the shared ASIN.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it to filter clearance products down to the ones that actually clear a resale ROI bar.

novel_features.name: Weekly price-drop ranking
novel_features.description: Rank the products that fell the most over the last week (or N weeks) across everything synced, by both dollar and percent drop.
novel_features.rationale: A weekly delta compares two points in a time series the live API only returns one product at a time; the store holds every product's history together to rank drops across the whole set.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for it to catch fresh markdowns first instead of re-pulling each product's history by hand.

novel_features.name: Retailer & category discount leaderboard
novel_features.description: Show which retailers and categories are consistently the deepest-discount buckets in your synced data, by average discount, on-sale count, or average price.
novel_features.rationale: A cross-retailer group-by aggregation the per-retailer API never computes; the store is the only place all retailers' products coexist to roll up.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it to decide which retailer/category to scan first when hunting deals.

novel_features.name: Price watch tracker
novel_features.description: Pin items you care about and, after each index refresh, see which ones moved, by how much, and which hit your target price.
novel_features.rationale: 'Has this changed since I last looked' is a stateful question the stateless API cannot answer; only the local store retains the prior observed price to diff against.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it for change-since-last-observation on a curated set instead of re-fetching and eyeballing prices.

narrative.headline: Every LemmeBuyIt retail endpoint, plus a local store that compares one UPC across 70+ retailers, ranks weekly price drops, and computes Amazon FBA arbitrage margin — compound queries the per-retailer API can't answer.
narrative.value_prop: Shopping wraps the LemmeBuyIt aggregated-retail API (115M+ products across 70+ retailers) and adds a local SQLite layer no other tool has. `index` syncs products and price history; `compare` finds the cheapest retailer for a shared identifier; `deals` runs a compound discount/price/rating/stock query across stores; `arbitrage` ranks Amazon FBA resale margin; `price-drops` surfaces weekly price drops and `leaderboard` ranks discount buckets. Agent-native --json/--select, offline search, typed exit codes.
narrative.auth_narrative: Authenticate with a single LemmeBuyIt API key sent as the `X-API-Key` header on every request (set `SHOPPING_API_KEY`, or run `shopping-pp-cli auth set-token <key>`). Free keys reach the `/shopping/` product surface; paid keys additionally unlock full product search, weekly price history, Amazon ASIN history, and the Amazon profitability data that powers `arbitrage`. Get a key at https://www.lemmebuyit.com/developer.

quickstart.comment: Verify config and connectivity before pulling data.
See which retailers your key can reach.
Build the local store from one retailer's on-sale catalog.
Run a compound deal query over the synced store.
Find the cheapest retailer for a shared identifier.

troubleshoots.fix: Set SHOPPING_API_KEY (or run `shopping-pp-cli auth set-token <key>`); every endpoint needs the X-API-Key header. Get a key at lemmebuyit.com/developer.

troubleshoots.symptom: 403 / 'not in your plan' on full products, price-history, or arbitrage
troubleshoots.fix: Those are paid-tier endpoints; a free key only reaches the `/shopping/` surface. Use a paid key or the `retailers shopping products` commands.
Run `shopping-pp-cli index --retailer <id>` first; these commands read the local store, which is empty until you sync.

narrative.when_to_use: Use Shopping for cross-retailer retail-product tasks an agent needs answered fast: find the cheapest retailer for a UPC, surface the best in-stock deals over a discount/price threshold, rank biggest weekly price drops, or evaluate Amazon FBA resale margin. It is strongest when you sync once and run many compound queries against the local store.
anti_triggers: Do not use it to place orders, add to cart, or check out — it is a read-only data API.
Do not use it for non-retail data or for a single retailer's own order-management API.
Do not use it as a real-time price oracle — store-backed queries reflect the last `index` sync, not the live shelf price.

recipes.title: Cheapest retailer for a UPC
recipes.explanation: Cross-retailer compare narrowed to the two fields an agent needs to act on.

recipes.title: Best in-stock deals
recipes.explanation: Compound discount/price/stock query ranked by discount over the synced store.

recipes.title: FBA arbitrage shortlist
recipes.explanation: Rank resale candidates by ROI, selecting only the decision fields.

recipes.title: This week's biggest drops
recipes.explanation: Weekly time-series delta ranking across every synced product.

trigger_phrases: find the cheapest retailer for this UPC
is this product profitable to flip on Amazon
show this week's biggest price drops
